notes: Per-CLI flags injected into the WizardCLIRunner extraArgs for the mini-app auto-fix path (autoFixMiniAppWithCLI in AIHarness+MiniAppOps.swift). Lets us pin a fast model per CLI without rebuilding mChatAI+ — ship via mchatai-source PR + refreshMchataisourceCache tunnel command. Phase ROUTE+2, see project_phase_route_cli_first_autofix memory. Phase 0 (2026-07-13): `perCLI` governs the AUTOFIX surface ONLY. Full feature/redesign iterates (Iterate button, typed follow-up, hub-URL remix) read `perSurface.iterate`; when absent they use the CLI default model and do NOT inherit the autofix haiku pin.

claude.rationale: Phase 0 (2026-07-13): feature/redesign iterates use the CLI default model. The autofix `--model haiku` pin was benchmarked ONLY for tiny single-file JS fixes; it was silently starving full iterates (the chaos-chess redesign ran on haiku). Leave empty (CLI default) unless a per-model iterate benchmark says otherwise; this is the zero-rebuild lever to change it.

gemini.rationale: Model selection is the StepwiseRunner planner's job; no CLI flag.

claude.rationale: Texas Hold em canary 2026-06-01: opus default 83s with maxTurns-reached + 11KB thinking; sonnet 187s with 11KB thinking; haiku 63s with 8KB thinking (best of three). `--effort low` was tried and made it WORSE (111s, 17KB thinking — possible cache-state interference). claude is rarely reached in practice because codex (25–55s, no extended thinking) runs first in the cascade and usually succeeds. This pin is a marginal improvement for the claude-runs-second case.

codex.rationale: Default codex CLI model already finishes the canary in 25–55s and 4/4 canary runs returned a successful 100%-size edit. No pin until a benchmark shows a faster alternative.

gemini.rationale: Antigravity routes through StepwiseRunner (per cli-invocation.json orchestration.mode=stepwise). Model selection is the stepwise planner's job, not a CLI flag. Leaving extraArgs empty avoids passing flags Stepwise would ignore.
